exam_price_notes: $93 exam fee + ~$30 test center fee. Fee waivers for military/low-income.
exam_rules: No calculator is allowed for Section 1. An online graphing calculator (non-CAS) is allowed for Section 2. Students are expected to know how and when to make appropriate use of the calculator.
target_audience: Students who have completed a one-semester college course in calculus
recommended_experience: Knowledge of preparatory mathematics, including algebra, geometry, trigonometry, and analytic geometry

prerequisites.description: Knowledge of preparatory mathematics, including algebra, geometry, trigonometry, and analytic geometry

retake_policy.notes: Each institution reserves the right to set its own credit-granting policy, which may differ from the American Council on Education (ACE). Contact your college to find out the score required for credit and the number of credit hours granted.

objectives.title: Statement of properties, e.g., limit of a constant, sum, product or quotient
Limit calculations, including limits involving infinity
The Derivative
sub_objectives: Definitions of the derivative
Derivatives of elementary functions
Derivatives of sums, products and quotients
Derivative of a composite function (chain rule)
Implicit differentiation
Derivative of the inverse of a function
Higher order derivatives
Corresponding characteristics of graphs of f, f', and f''
Statement of the Mean Value Theorem; applications and graphical illustrations
Relation between differentiability and continuity
Use of L'Hospital's Rule (quotient and indeterminate forms)

objectives.title: Applications of the Derivative
sub_objectives: Slope of a curve at a point
Tangent lines and linear approximation
Curve sketching: increasing and decreasing functions; relative and absolute maximum and minimum points; concavity; points of inflection
Extreme value problems
Velocity and acceleration of a particle moving along a line
Average and instantaneous rates of change
Related rates of change

objectives.title: Antiderivatives and Techniques of Integration
sub_objectives: Concept of antiderivatives
Basic integration formulas
Integration by substitution (use of identities and change of variable)

objectives.title: Applications of Antiderivatives
sub_objectives: Distance and velocity from acceleration with initial conditions
Solutions of y' = ky and applications to growth and decay

objectives.title: The Definite Integral
sub_objectives: Definition of the definite integral as the limit of a sequence of Riemann sums and approximations of the definite integral using areas of rectangles
Properties of the definite integral
The Fundamental Theorem

objectives.title: Applications of the Definite Integral
sub_objectives: Average value of a function on an interval
Area, including area between curves
Other (e.g., accumulated change from a rate of change)

official_study_resources.title: CLEP Calculus Examination Guide
official_study_resources.notes: The Calculus exam is approximately 60% limits and differential calculus and 40% integral calculus.

official_study_resources.title: Study Resources: Calculus
official_study_resources.notes: A study plan and list of online resources.

official_study_resources.title: Sample Questions: Calculus
official_study_resources.notes: Answer sample questions related to the Calculus exam.

official_study_resources.title: What Your CLEP Score Means
official_study_resources.notes: Guide to understanding how CLEP scores are calculated and credit-granting recommendations for all exams.

official_study_resources.title: ACE Credit Recommendations
official_study_resources.notes: Recommendations for credit-granting scores from the American Council on Education.
